rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Proposed Revised Second Amended Verified Petition; Memorandium Of Points And Authorities; Declarrtion Of Camille Colarusso
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Russell S. Roeca. Before the court is Petitioner Camille Colarusso's Motion for Leave to File a Proposed Revised Second Amended Verified Petition. No timely opposition to the Motion has been filed. The Motion is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART, as follows. As background, on September 29, 2025, Ms. Colarusso filed Second Amended Verified Petition For: Breach Of Fiduciary Duty-Constructive Fraud/Fraudulent Concealment Aiding And Abetting Breach Of Fiduciary Duty-Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Against Jon Vigna For Failure To Remedy Breaches Of Michael Rooney-Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Against Michael Rooney Sole Special Administrator/Co-Executor On Surety Bond-Extrinsic Fraud-Restitution Surcharge Removal Of Executor And Appointment Of Successor To Set Aside Final Judgment ("Second Amended Petition"). On November 3, 2025, John Vigna, as Executor, filed Response and Opposition to Second Amended Petition. On December 11, 2025, this court granted Respondents John D. Minton and Asher Anderson's Motion to Quash Service of the Second Amended Petition. This Motion was filed on December 30, 2025. Petitioner's declaration in support of the Motion states it is in response to that Order, seeking to correct service issues. Notice of Errata regarding notice of this Motion - submitting a corrected DE-120 proof of service page - was filed January 13, 2026. Per the Notice, the parties were served, but the service list was not included in the initial filing. Petitioner now seeks leave of court to file an amended petition pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 473(a), and also to maintain the current hearing date of March 30, 2026. The request that the March 30, 2026 hearing proceed is DENIED, as moot. Future hearings in this matter are currently April 14, 2026 and May 13, 2026. The court will address notice of this Motion as a threshold issue. As stated in Declaration of Camille Colarusso Regarding Service Attempts and Courtesy Notice for Notice of Motion and Motion for Leave to File a Proposed Revised Second Amended Verified Petition, Movant attempted to serve Raymond P. Smith and Jonathan Knott by international mail in September 2025, at an address outside of the country, and the mailing were returned marked as "unclaimed" refused not wanted" and "gone away moved." In January, Movant again attempted to serve Smith and Knott by mail at the same address. Per Movant's declaration, those mailings have not been returned undeliverable. Per her Declaration, Movant has also attempted to provide actual notice via email sent to Mr. Smith and Mr. Knott, and by serving Mr. Smith's and Mr. Knott's attorney of record by mail, as reflected in the Proof of Service. Hearing on the Motion was previously continued by the court, on the understanding that Movant was continuing her attempts to serve Mr. Smith and Mr. Knott by mail at either of their last known addresses, or by some other means, and to allow sufficient time for notice served internationally (see Code of Civ. Proc. sec. 1005, additional time).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Of the responding parties named in the proposed amendment, only John Vigna, as Executor, has filed a response to the currently-pending Second Amended Petition. There is no clear showing that parties named in the proposed amendment would be substantially prejudiced by allowing Petitioner to remove as respondents the attorneys named in the Second Amended Petition (John D. Minton, Asher Anderson, Shiela Robello, Vincent Yu, and Yulissa Zuliaka). There is also no clear showing that parties named in the proposed amendment would be substantially prejudiced by allowing Petitioner to remove certain causes of action. After consideration of the proposed amended petition in comparison to the Second Amended Petition, the court in its discretion also gives Petitioner leave to amend the petition to include the two facially new causes of action, under Probate Code sections 850 and 859 and for an estate accounting. The allegations underlying the new causes of action were pleaded in the Second Amended Petition. There is no clear showing that parties named in the proposed amendment would be substantially prejudiced by allowing Petitioner to formally plead these causes of action. There is currently no trial or evidentiary hearing date set in this matter. The Petitioner has the burden of proof on the claims. Petitioner is directed to file and serve the proposed amended petition attached as an exhibit to the Motion, within seven (7) days of entry of this Order. After it is filed, the court will continue the hearing on the further amended petition to allow adequate time for notice. Petitioner, Ms. Colarusso, shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] Now, upon review of the record, the court finds notice of the Motion has been given in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 1015, as it appears that Mr. Smith and Mr. Knott are no longer residing in California, and the papers were served upon Mr. Smith's and Mr. Knott's attorney of record, at his address of record. ("But in all cases where a [non-resident] party has an attorney in the action or proceeding, the service of papers, when required, must be upon the attorney instead of the party, except service of subpoenas, of writs, and other process issued in the suit, and of papers to bring the party into contempt." Code of Civil Proc. sec. 1015.) Substantively, California courts have a policy of great liberality in allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage of the proceeding so as to dispose of cases upon their substantial merits where the authorization does not prejudice the substantial rights of others. "A trial court has "wide discretion" in allowing the amendment of any pleading, and as a matter of policy the ruling of the trial court in such matters will be upheld unless a "manifest or gross abuse of discretion is shown." (Mac v. Minassian (2022) 76 Cal. App. 5th 510, 516.) Specifically as to notice, Code of Civil Procedure section 473(a)(1) consists of two clauses. The first clause allows the court to allow a party to correct mistakes and add or remove parties' names from the pleading "in furtherance of justice, and on any terms as may be proper." The second clause, facially, requires notice to the adverse party. The first clause states: "The court may, in furtherance of justice, and on any terms as may be proper, allow a party to amend any pleading or proceeding by adding or striking out the name of any party, or by correcting a mistake in the name of a party, or a mistake in any other respect[.]" The second clause states, "[the court] may likewise, in its discretion, after notice to the adverse party, allow, upon any terms as may be just, an amendment to any pleading or proceeding in other particulars; and may upon like terms allow an answer to be made after the time limited by this code." The Motion states Petitioner seeks leave to amend "in order to correct procedural defects identified during Motion practice, remove improperly named respondents, and proceed solely against proper fiduciaries within the Probate Courts Jurisdiction [sic]." (MPA at 3:4-7.) The proposed amended petition is attached as an exhibit to the Motion. The proposed amended petition would remove as respondents the attorneys named in the Second Amended Petition (John D. Minton, Asher Anderson, Shiela Robello, Vincent Yu, and Yulissa Zuliaka). In addition to removing certain people as respondents, the proposed amendment attached as an exhibit to the Motion would also make substantive changes to the Second Amended Petition's causes of action. The factual allegations remain essentially the same between the two versions. But the total number of causes of action listed on the face of the pleading changes from twelve in the Second Amended Petition to seven in the proposed amendment. The following causes of action are not included in the proposed amendment: to remove the executor and appoint a successor, to surcharge all respondents, and the breach of duty claims against specific respondents in their fiduciary capacities. Two new causes of action are pleaded on the face of the proposed amendment: damages under Probate Code sections 850 and 859, and to compel an estate accounting. The prayer is the same between the two versions, except "removal of Respondents as fiduciaries" is not included in the proposed amended petition's prayer for relief. The Motion does not address whether others will or will not be prejudiced by such amendments.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVELYN JUNG WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/3/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 3/20/26 shows that notice was mailed on 11/8/25 for a hearing on 1/8/25. It also did not have a copy of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice of hearing. (2) See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. The supplement (that amended the petition) did not include the fiduciary's statement verifying that the statement was received in electronic form and printed without alteration. (3) All the funds exceeding the FDIC insured amount are apparently still held at one bank. (4) Costs of sale are not a "loss" on sale. Does the executor wish to amend the fee request again? (5) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] The sole beneficiary here is the trustee. An amendment must filed to amend the prayer with the guardian ad litem fee that was not in the petition prayer and the amended amount of the statutory attorney fee shown in the "supplement" filed on 3/20/26 (or to request a greater statutory fee). After the amendment is e-filed, notice with a copy of the remote appearance instructions attached it must be given for the hearing on 6/3/26 at least 15 days before that date. Please provide courtesy copies of the amendment and the proof of service to the assigned examiner by 5/22/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAY EARL GOODMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The fee declaration was not signed and verified by the attorney and has the wrong case number in the caption. 2) Accessing a safe deposit box (even if drilling is necessary), terminating a lease due to the death of the tenant, and reviewing tax returns prepared by a CPA are the ordinary duties of a personal representative. If the attorney had to perform them, the personal representative's compensation should be reduced. 3) The attorney claims that there were "nearly ten financial institutions to contact," but the I&As show just six financial institutions. Again, contacting numerous financial institutions is the duty of a personal representative. 4) The CPA reportedly suggested a worst-case scenario of potential tax liability of $470,000, but the administrator is requesting to withhold $518,288.93. 5) The attorney costs of $2,493.40 have not been itemized. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RUBLY FAMILY TRUST DATED JULY 20, 1988, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 4] Exhibit A to both Declaration of Elizabeth Brenner In Opposition To Motion To Quash or For Protective Order and Declaration of Ryan Meckfessel In Opposition To Motion To Quash or For Protective Order includes a Notice To Consumer Or Employee And Objection (form SUBP-025) directed to Ellen Crabaugh, regarding the subpoena directed to Frost Bank, and executed November 17, 2025, with proof of service by mail completed November 17, 2025 and signed under penalty of perjury. The address listed on the proof of service is the same address listed for Ms. Crabaugh on Application to Probate a Copy of a Lost Will as a Muniment of Title filed February 2019, attached as part of Exhibit 5 to the Motion. Exhibit 1 to the Motion is Deposition Subpoena for Production of Business Records (form SUBP-010), directed to Frost Bank, at an address in Austin, Texas, executed on November 17, 2025, with a deposition production date of December 18, 2025. The proof of service page is blank. Also in Exhibit 1 is a Subpoena issued by the 34th District Court of Travis County, Texas, directed at Frost Bank, at the same address in Austin, Texas, with the November 17, 2025 California SUBP-010 form attached, executed and issued by the Texas court clerk on January 27, 2026, with an appearance/production date of March 3, 2026. Exhibit 3 to the Motion is Notice of Intention to Take Deposition Upon Written Questions and Issue Subpoena Duces Tecum of a Financial Institution, issued by the 34th District Court of Travis County, Texas, and executed attorney Ms. Brenner on January 29, 2026. Attached to the Notice is a Certificate of Service listing Marshal A. Oldman as counsel for Mr. Crabaugh, and the Subpoena issued by the Texas court. Attached as part of the Subpoena is a document titled Direct Questions to be Propounded to the Witness, a copy of the November 17, 2025 California SUBP-010 subpoena form. The Motion does not argue that the Notice was served to the wrong address, but that Movant was not served at all. It appears the November 17, 2025 Notice to Consumer (SUBP-025) was sent to Ms. Crabaugh's last known address, as listed in the paperwork Ms. Crabaugh filed in connection with decedent's death and estate administration, which is Code-compliant service under California law. It also appears from the record before the court that Ms. Crabaugh's attorney of record was served notice of the subpoena on January 29, 2026, via the Texas court-issued documents discussed above. Service of notice of subpoena on a party's attorney is also Code-compliant, under California law. (See, Code of Civil Procedure sec. 1983.5.) Texas Rules of Civil Procedure, Rule 176.6, the specific rule cited in the Notice of Motion, pertains to responses to subpoenas. After review of the Texas Rules of Civil Procedure cited by Movant and in the opposition, the court agrees with Texas counsel Ms. Brenner, that the subpoena was properly noticed and served on Frost Bank under Texas Rules of Civil Procedure (see, TRCP, rules 205.3, 205.1, 205.2, 201.3, 176.5).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RUBLY FAMILY TRUST DATED JULY 20, 1988, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Subpoena To Frost Bank Or, In The Alternative, For Protective Order;
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 4] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Russell S. Roeca. Before the court is Interested Party Ellen Crabaugh's Motion to Quash Subpoena to Frost Bank or, in the alternative, for Protective Order ("Motion"). After hearing on April 1, 2026, the subpoenaing party was appointed Temporary Successor Trustee of the Rubly Family Trust, dated July 20, 1988. The court found that it had jurisdiction over this trust, as it is a California trust and, per the record before the court, assets titled to this trust are currently held and managed by a financial investment company located in San Francisco, California. Movant's evidentiary objections to the declaration of Elizabeth Brenner submitted with the opposition to motion are OVERRULED. Movant's evidentiary objections to the declaration of Ryan Meckfessel submitted with the opposition to motion are OVERRULED. The Motion is DENIED. Per the Notice of Motion, the Motion is brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 1985.3 and 1987.1 and Texas Rules of Civil Procedure, Rule 176.6, seeking to quash the subpoena on the grounds that it seeks private and irrelevant records, is impermissibly overbroad and without limitation, invasive of third parties' privacy rights, and is harassing and oppressive. In the alternative, the Motion seeks a protective order, under Code of Civil Procedure section 1987.1(a). Movant Ms. Crabaugh's declaration in support of the Motion states she was not served Notice to Consumer (SUBP-025) or a copy of the underlying subpoena (Crabaugh Decl. at pars. 8-11). Declaration of attorney Susan B. Rosenblat states counsel for Ms. Crabaugh was served a copy of the Subpoena on or about January 29, 2026 and counsel executed and returned a rejection of waiver of notice included with the Subpoena on February 3, 2026 (attached as Exhibit 7 to the Motion). Under California Code of Civil Procedure section 1985.3(b), the consumer whose personal records are sought must be served a copy of the subpoena, the Notice to Consumer form (SUBP-025), and "proof of service as indicated in paragraph (1) of subdivision (c)" of section 1985.3. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2020.410(c), "A deposition subpoena that commands only the production of business records for copying need not be accompanied by an affidavit or declaration showing good cause for the production of the business records designated in it." Service of the required documents may be either personally, or at the consumer's last known address, or in accordance with Code of Civil Procedure section 1010 et seq., or, if the consumer is a party, to the attorney of record. This service must occur at least five days prior to service on the custodian of records, and not less than ten days prior to the date of production specified in the subpoena, plus any additional time required for service by mail under Section 1013.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RUBLY FAMILY TRUST DATED JULY 20, 1988, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 4] Substantively, Movant also seeks to quash the subpoena, or in the alternative, seeks a protective order, on grounds that it is overbroad, unlimited in time, seeks confidential bank records irrelevant to the pending petition, and violates decedent's and Crabaugh's right of privacy to their communications with Frost Bank. Under Code of Civil Procedure sec. 1987.1(a), If a subpoena requires the attendance of a witness or the production of books, documents, electronically stored information, or other things before a court, or at the trial of an issue therein, or at the taking of a deposition, the court, upon motion reasonably made by any person described in subdivision (b), or upon the court's own motion after giving counsel notice and an opportunity to be heard, may make an order quashing the subpoena entirely, modifying it, or directing compliance with it upon those terms or conditions as the court shall declare, including protective orders. In addition, the court may make any other order as may be appropriate to protect the person from unreasonable or oppressive demands, including unreasonable violations of the right of privacy of the person. The court does not find that the instant subpoena's document requests are unreasonable or oppressive in the context of the instant matter. Petitioner's underlying petition to be appointed successor trustee of the Rubly Family Trust, dated July 20, 1988, was previously granted. The subpoena seeks all records and communications in Frost Bank's possession regarding that trust. That information is discoverable. Per Movant's statements in this Motion and in the opposition to the underlying petition, the 1988 Trust was superseded by the Barbara Rubly Family Trust dated May 2, 2011, and was intended to replace the 1988 Trust. The Barbara Rubly Grandchildrens Trust dated May 2, 2011 was created at the same time. Decedent was settlor and initial trustee of both 2011 trusts, and the surviving settlor and trustee of the 1988 Trust. Movant is successor trustee of both 2011 trusts. The subpoenaed records and communications regarding the 2011 Trusts and decedent and Movant are reasonably within the broad scope of discoverable information under the California Discovery Act (2017.010). As for the privilege objections, it is not clear from the Motion whether Movant is raising this in her capacity as successor trustee of the 2011 trusts, or in her personal capacity. The subpoenaed documents and information about the 2011 trusts is directly relevant to accounting for the assets and management of the 1988 Trust, before and after the 2011 Trusts were established. As successor trustee of the Barbara Rubly Family Trust dated May 2, 2011 and the Barbara Rubly Grandchildrens [sic] Trust dated May 2, 2011, Ms. Crabaugh's personal financial information should not be intermingled in the records of the 1988 Trust or the 2011 trusts. For the first time in the Reply, Movant states she also, separately served objections to the subpoena on Petitioner's counsel pursuant to Texas Rules of Civil Procedure, and Petitioner failed to bring a timely motion to compel a consumer's personal records under California Code of Civil Procedure section 1987.1. This basis for the Motion was not presented in the Notice of Motion, and thus will not be considered.
